danger_signals.visual_form: Shellcreepers, Sidesteppers, Fighter Flies, Slipice creatures visually distinct from environment, moving from corner pipes toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; contact with upright enemy = loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite in normal standing/walking posture on platforms
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy is dangerous; touching it causes death

danger_signals.visual_form: Floating projectile sprites that traverse the screen independently of platforms
danger_signals.meaning: Airborne hazard; contact = death; cannot be flipped like grounded enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: Crystalline visual accumulation on platform bottoms in later rounds
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard forming over time; fully formed icicles cause death on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Flipped enemy (on back) gradually returning to upright orientation
danger_signals.meaning: Time pressure: window to eliminate enemy is closing; if enemy stands back up, hazard is restored

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite inverted/upside-down on platform surface
safety_signals.meaning: Enemy is temporarily neutralized and vulnerable; player can safely approach and eliminate by kicking

safety_signals.visual_form: Illuminated POW block sprite centered on level, glowing/active appearance
safety_signals.meaning: Area control tool available; activating flips all platform-touching enemies instantly, providing momentary safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Platform surfaces rendered without any enemy sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal space; no immediate threat in that zone

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating coin sprites exiting pipes after enemies defeated
safety_signals.meaning: Enemy successfully eliminated; bonus area is now safe and points are available

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red (Mario) and Green (Luigi) overalls with distinguishing hat/sprite colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Plumber aesthetic: operates within sewer pipe network of New York City

faction_indicators.color_identity: Shellcreepers (brown/turtle-like), Sidesteppers (red crabs), Fighter Flies (dark), Slipice (ice-colored)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each creature type has distinct silhouette and movement pattern; visual diversity indicates variety of threat types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Emerge from corner pipes; visually anomalous to the urban sewer platform environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer enemy types on screen; platforms appear clean without icicle formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies move at moderate pace; no environmental hazards beyond creature threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy density; POW block still available (shows charge state visually)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawn rate increases; creatures become more aggressive in emergence timing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visible icicle accumulation on platform undersides; increased fireball presence; POW block depleted/non-glowing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple simultaneous threats; icicles fall progressively; enemy types rotate rapidly; player must manage compound hazards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All enemies defeated; screen transitions or level indicator shows progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creature spawning ceases; new phase begins with increased difficulty parameters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite removed from screen; game-over text or screen-wipe transition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All entity movement ceases; no further input response except restart

environmental_storytelling.example: The four corner pipes establish the location as a subsurface sewage system. Enemies emerge from these pipes, implying an infestation originating from underground infrastructure. The sewage context narratively justifies the creature types (aquatic/subterranean beings) and establishes the plumber protagonist's occupational role as logical.

Multi-level horizontal platforms with screen-edge wraparound suggest a confined section of sewer tunnel or chamber. The confined space with limited vertical/horizontal extent creates the gameplay loop: enemies spawn, player must manage them within the bounded area, implying this is a containment/clearing operation.

Coins emerge from the same pipes that spawn enemies, suggesting enemy defeat triggers a secondary mechanism in the pipe system. This visual association implies the plumber's action of clearing creatures somehow activates the coin reward system—narrative justification for score mechanics.

Gradual crystalline buildup on platform undersides in later rounds indicates environmental degradation or freezing conditions spreading through the sewer. Visual progression of ice accumulation conveys escalating environmental hazard unrelated to creature threats, implying an expanding crisis.

The POW block at screen center serves as the spatial and narrative anchor: it's the player's strategic tool to manage the creature outbreak. Its central placement and limited uses (three activations) suggest it's a critical emergency device with finite capacity, implying urgency and resource management in the containment scenario.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Crawls across platforms horizontally; predictable patrol movement; can only be flipped when struck from below
Struggles/thrashes upside-down; gradually rights itself if not kicked away; vulnerable to removal
Moves laterally with distinct side-crab gait; same flipping vulnerability as Shellcreeper
Jumps vertically between platforms; cannot be flipped mid-air; only vulnerable when landing on platform
Transforms platform surface into slippery ice; changes player movement physics; can be flipped when contacted from below
Floats freely across screen independent of platforms; no flipping mechanic; constant hazard state; cannot be eliminated by standard jump/kick
Emits visual effect; instantly flips all contact-platform enemies; block becomes inert after three uses; visual deactivation indicates resource depletion
Can move freely; can strike platforms below to flip enemies; can activate POW block
Immediate death; character sprite removed; life counter decrements; respawn or game-over sequence triggers

notes: Mario Bros. (NES 1986) uses visual clarity and spatial arrangement to communicate danger and safety states. Enemies are distinguished by unique sprite designs that immediately signal threat type (grounded vs. airborne, speed, special properties like ice). The sewer platform environment provides narrative context for the infestation scenario. Danger signals are primarily organism-based (enemy presence and orientation), while safety emerges from enemy incapacitation states. The progression system (increasing enemy density, icicle formation) is conveyed through visual accumulation rather than UI elements, relying on player observation of screen state. The game lacks explicit faction symbolism but uses occupational/contextual storytelling (plumbers, sewers, pipes) to establish player agency. No political or ideological dimensions are present; the world-state is purely mechanical/ecological.
